title: Generation and Assembly of Forebrain Spheroids
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bsmby","text":"Here we show the generation and assembly from human pluripotent stem (hPS) cells of subdomain-specific forebrain spheroids: human cortical spheroids (hCS) and human subpallial spheroids (hSS). This protocol describes hPS cell maintenance, neural spheroid generation, patterning and assembly to obtain functionally-integrated human forebrain 3D cultures.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: This protocol details the assembly of human forebrain spheroids, which mimic some aspects of the human forebrain development.
Ensure you have all reagents prepared. Set up your work area to maintain sterile conditions throughout the experiment.
Thaw human pluripotent stem cells (hPSCs) quickly in a 37°C water bath.
Transfer cells into a 15 mL conical tube containing 10 mL warm mTeSR1 medium.
Centrifuge at 200 x g for 5 minutes at room temperature.
Carefully aspirate the supernatant without disturbing the cell pellet.
Resuspend cells in 5 mL fresh mTeSR1 medium. Count cells using a hemocytometer.
Plate the desired number of cells in a 6-well plate coated with Matrigel.
Maintain the cells in a humidified incubator at 37°C with 5% CO2.
Change the medium every other day until the experiment is terminated.
Monitor differentiation progress under a microscope daily.
Discard any batches if contamination is suspected.
No competing financial interests declared. *Fikri Birey & Jimena Andersen contributed equally to this work.

Assembly of functionally integrated human forebrain spheroids by Fikri Birey, Jimena Andersen, Christopher D. Makinson, Saiful Islam, Wu Wei, Nina Huber, H. Christina Fan, Kimberly R. Cordes Metzler, Georgia Panagiotakos, Nicholas Thom, Nancy A. O’Rourke, Lars M. Steinmetz, Jonathan A. Bernstein, Joachim Hallmayer, Joh published in Nature on 27 Apr, 2017 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nature22330
